req.title: Account close (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall close an account with zero balance; closing with non-zero balance shall require sweep to another account. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers account closure under multiple conditions: zero-balance happy path, non-zero balance without sweep (409), non-zero balance with valid sweep, unauthorized caller, and boundary at exactly zero cents. Tests are partitioned by: (1) EP – zero vs non-zero balance classes; (2) STC – account state transitions (active→closed); (3) NEG – unauthorized caller and missing sweep; (4) BVA – balance exactly at zero boundary (0.00); (5) DT – decision table combining balance flag × sweep flag × auth flag. Test[5] (close already-closed account) is a negative test exercising an invalid state-transition partition; the heuristic is corrected from EP to STC because the distinguishing factor is the account's current state in the state transition cycle, not an equivalence class of input data values. Each test uses isolated fixture accounts to ensure idempotency.

tests.name: test_close_account_zero_balance_succeeds
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/close with valid owner auth token
tests.executable:
def test_close_account_zero_balance_succeeds(zero_balance_account, auth_headers):
    account_id = zero_balance_account["id"]
    resp = <api_client>.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/close",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "closed"
    assert "final_statement_url" in body
    assert body["final_statement_url"] is not None

tests.name: test_close_account_nonzero_balance_no_sweep_returns_409
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/close with valid owner auth token and no sweep payload
Assert error code indicates sweep required
trace: R-FIN-064: non-zero balance without sweep yields 409
tests.executable:
def test_close_account_nonzero_balance_no_sweep_returns_409(nonzero_balance_account, auth_headers):
    account_id = nonzero_balance_account["id"]
    resp = <api_client>.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/close",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["error"]["code"] == "SWEEP_REQUIRED"
    status_resp = <api_client>.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert status_resp.json()["state"] == "active"

tests.name: test_close_account_balance_exactly_zero_boundary
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/close with valid owner auth token
trace: R-FIN-064: boundary at balance=0.00 must succeed without sweep
R-FIN-064: BVA lower bound of non-zero partition
tests.executable:
def test_close_account_balance_exactly_zero_boundary(zero_balance_account, auth_headers):
    account_id = zero_balance_account["id"]
    detail = <api_client>.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}", headers=auth_headers).json()
    assert float(detail["balance"]) == 0.00, "Precondition: balance must be exactly 0.00"
    resp = <api_client>.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/close",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["state"] == "closed"

tests.name: test_close_account_unauthorized_caller_returns_403
preconditions: Account is owned by customer A
Caller authenticates as customer B (different owner)
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/close using customer B auth token
tests.executable:
def test_close_account_unauthorized_caller_returns_403(zero_balance_account, other_customer_headers, auth_headers):
    account_id = zero_balance_account["id"]
    resp = <api_client>.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/close",
        headers=other_customer_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["error"]["code"] == "UNAUTHORIZED"
    status_resp = <api_client>.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert status_resp.json()["state"] == "active"

tests.name: test_close_account_nonzero_balance_with_valid_sweep_succeeds
preconditions: Authenticated customer owns both source and destination accounts
Destination account is active and accepts deposits
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/close with sweep_to_account_id in request body
Assert destination account balance increased by 250.00
Assert final statement generated for source account
expected: destination account balance incremented by transfer amount
trace: R-FIN-064: non-zero balance with sweep closes successfully
R-FIN-064: DT rule balance!=0 AND sweep_provided => success
tests.executable:
def test_close_account_nonzero_balance_with_valid_sweep_succeeds(
    nonzero_balance_account, sweep_destination_account, auth_headers
):
    src_id = nonzero_balance_account["id"]
    dst_id = sweep_destination_account["id"]
    pre_dst = <api_client>.get(f"/accounts/{dst_id}", headers=auth_headers).json()
    pre_balance = float(pre_dst["balance"])
    transfer_amount = float(nonzero_balance_account["balance"])
    resp = <api_client>.post(
        f"/accounts/{src_id}/close",
        headers=auth_headers,
        json={"sweep_to_account_id": dst_id},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "closed"
    assert "final_statement_url" in body
    post_dst = <api_client>.get(f"/accounts/{dst_id}", headers=auth_headers).json()
    assert float(post_dst["balance"]) == pytest.approx(pre_balance + transfer_amount, abs=0.01)

tests.name: test_close_already_closed_account_returns_409
actions: Assert error indicates invalid state transition
trace: R-FIN-064: STC invalid transition closed->closed must be rejected
R-FIN-064: state transition from closed to closed must be rejected
tests.executable:
def test_close_already_closed_account_returns_409(closed_account, auth_headers):
    account_id = closed_account["id"]
    resp = <api_client>.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/close",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["error"]["code"] in ("INVALID_STATE_TRANSITION", "ACCOUNT_ALREADY_CLOSED")
